<insights>

- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
- Perfect cohesion (1.0) and zero coupling reflects that this zone is a pure static asset group with no runtime dependencies — exactly correct for an HTML shell.
- The dual logo files (lightmode/darkmode) indicate theme-aware branding is baked into the asset layer rather than handled at runtime, which is appropriate for static delivery.
- No entry points are detected, which is expected — the HTML file is loaded directly by the browser rather than imported by any JavaScript module.
- Zero coupling and perfect cohesion confirm this zone is correctly scoped as a static shell; no action needed.
- Keeping the HTML shell and logo assets co-located in one zone makes it easy to update branding or swap the SPA entry point without touching production logic.
- viewer-shell's zero outbound imports confirm the SPA bootstrap pattern is correctly implemented: the HTML shell references a JS bundle entry point without any shared module-graph membership, keeping the static delivery layer completely decoupled from the application runtime.
- Zero coupling in both directions (no imports to or from any zone) is the expected and correct pattern for an HTML bootstrap shell — no action needed and serves as a reference for static asset zone hygiene.
- The zone name 'viewer-shell' is partially redundant: all three files already reside under src/viewer/, so the 'viewer-' prefix in the zone name conveys no additional location information. A name like 'spa-bootstrap' or 'app-shell' would better describe the zone's architectural role without echoing its directory path.
- Rename zone 'viewer-shell' to 'spa-bootstrap' or 'app-shell' to describe its bootstrap role rather than repeating its directory path prefix; zone names should convey purpose, not location.

</insights>
